name: Formatted text
description: Renders office documents and PDF/RTF as safe, minimal, semantic HTML via text-extractor. Covers the subset of text-extractor's supported formats that file-type actually detects a binary signature for (docx/pptx/xlsx/odt/odp/ods via their ZIP+internal-structure signature, pdf via its %PDF- header, rtf via its {\rtf1 header) — confirmed empirically, not assumed: file-handler-detect.js's detectMime() forces file_mode to "binary" whenever it finds a signature, regardless of how text-ish the format conceptually is. See formatted-document-plain/renderer.json for the sibling covering this same extractor's remaining formats (csv/md/html), which have no fixed binary signature and are classified file_mode "text" instead. Both manifests share the same tab name and the same underlying extractor deliberately — the split exists only because of this project's existing text-vs-binary classification, not because of anything different about how these two groups render.
